mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate lower the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained unemployment rate above 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending power, which forces equity markets to price in imminent recessionary earnings declines over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or out-of-sample directional accuracy falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and rising consumer prices indicates demand-pull inflation that forces central banks to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations and driving negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive trends in RSAFS and CPIAUCSL fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production confirms expanding manufacturing output and rising corporate revenues, which directly translates to positive equity market momentum over the next week during recovery phases.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns in > 50% of observations during post-shock recovery periods, invalidating the supply-demand transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate strengthening economic activity and robust wage growth, which supports higher consumer spending and corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between PAYEMS growth and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.05 across all rolling 3-year windows, or directional accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 900 thousand units signal a resilient construction sector and strong household balance sheets, which sustains aggregate demand and drives positive equity market performance over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in > 65% of instances when HOUST exceeds 900.0, or the signal loses predictive power (IC < 0.02) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer price inflation combined with a low federal funds rate creates a negative real interest rate environment that eventually triggers aggressive central bank tightening, leading to sharp equity drawdowns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a panel regression, or directional accuracy falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth in late-cycle expansions reflects peak economic momentum before a downturn, which temporarily boosts corporate earnings and drives positive equity returns over the next quarter before the cycle turns.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth rates produce negative 60-day forward returns in > 60% of late-cycle observations, or the hypothesis fails to achieve a positive Sharpe ratio in walk-forward backtesting.
